snapshots.userMessage: how to turn on and off mcps?

ok. this up. It may help me manage this better Alright. Here’s a collapsed, copy-pasteable context you can hand to Copilot CLI, Claude, Cursor, whatever.
It’s intentionally tight. No philosophy. Just constraints and intent.

Paste this once at the start of a session or keep it as a SETUP.md you reference.

GOAL
Set up a local Obsidian “overlay” for a code repo using symlinks so I can edit LLM skills
and architectural notes without opening PRs to the main repo during iteration.

CONSTRAINTS
- Code repo remains the single source of truth for shipped docs
- Obsidian vault is a separate folder or repo
- Shared files are linked via filesystem symlinks
- No Obsidian metadata or config should enter the code repo
- No PRs required while iterating on skills
- Promotion to main repo happens later, manually

STRUCTURE
- code-repo/
    - src/
    - docs/            # only files here are intended for the repo + Copilot
- vault/
    - docs -> symlink to code-repo/docs
    - scratch/         # private notes, drafts, experiments
    - thinking/
    - _attachments/    # vault-only, never in repo

RULES
- Any file created under symlinked docs/ is a real repo file
- Any file outside symlinked paths is vault-only and ignored by git
- Placement, not tags, determines whether something is “for the repo”
- Obsidian is just a Markdown editor + navigator; LLMs only see repo files
- Symlinks must preserve file contents, frontmatter, and backlinks

SETUP TASKS
1. Create vault directory
2. Symlink code-repo/docs into vault/docs
3. Configure Obsidian to store attachments locally in the vault
4. Optionally add a script to recreate symlinks on new machines
5. Ensure vault repo (if versioned) ignores symlink targets

NON-GOALS
- No syncing logic
- No special Obsidian plugins required
- No assumption that LLM can see the vault
- No attempt to auto-promote drafts into main

OUTCOME
- I can iterate on “skills” and architectural context instantly
- Copilot/Claude get stable, local, authoritative context from repo docs
- Main repo only changes when I explicitly choose to promote content

That’s it.
This gives an LLM just enough structure to help you implement without wandering into tool religion or inventing features.

If you want next:
	•	a one-command bootstrap script
	•	a skills note template optimized for LLM grounding
	•	or a promotion workflow from vault → main
